parameters.query: SELECT id, client_name, domain, keywords, country, engine FROM clients WHERE active = true ORDER BY id;

nodes.name: Split into one item per keyword
DataForSEO — fetch SERP
nodes.notes:
Replace with your preferred SERP API. ValueSERP alternative shown in comments below.

ValueSERP alternative URL:
https://api.valueserp.com/search?api_key=YOUR_KEY&q={{ $json.keyword }}&gl={{ $json.country }}&num=100

For ValueSERP, change HTTP method to GET and remove the body.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO rankings (client_id, keyword, rank, checked_at)
VALUES (
  {{ $json.client_id }},
  '{{ $json.keyword }}',
  {{ $json.rank !== null ? $json.rank : 'NULL' }},
  CURRENT_DATE
)
ON CONFLICT (client_id, keyword, checked_at)
DO UPDATE SET rank = EXCLUDED.rank
RETURNING id, client_id, keyword, rank, checked_at;

nodes.name: Send daily digest email
nodes.notes: Optional. Configure SMTP credentials in n8n settings. Remove this node if email summary not needed.

main.node: Split into one item per keyword

meta.description: Runs every morning at 07:00. Reads all active clients from the DB, checks keyword rankings via DataForSEO, and saves one row per keyword per day into the rankings table. Sends an optional email digest.
meta.notes:
SETUP:
1. Create a Postgres credential named 'Supabase Postgres'
2. Create a DataForSEO (or ValueSERP) HTTP Basic Auth credential
3. Update the SERP API node with your credential
4. (Optional) Configure SMTP for the email digest node
5. Activate the workflow
